title: Paracetamol
content_markdown:
##### Type

Simple analgesic / antipyretic 

##### Presentations

**PO:** 48mg/ml colour free liquid (oral) 
**IV:** 1g in 100ml in collapsible plastic flask (IV) 
**PO:** 500mg tablet (oral) 

##### Actions

- Antipyretic

- Mild—moderate analgesic

Onset Oral: 30 minutes 
 IV: 5-10 minutes 

#### Uses

- Pyrexia in children who have had or may have a febrile convulsion (for ACTAS treatment, pyrexia is generally >38\xb0C)

- Mild to moderate pain (oral)

- Moderate to severe pain (IV)

#### Adverse Effects

Rare – none of these side effects have been confirmed or refuted as being linked to the casual use of paracetamol:

- Dyspepsia

- Nausea

- Allergic reactions

- Haematological reactions

- Dizziness

- Pain at injection site

#### Contraindications

- Known or suspected allergy to paracetamol

- Previous paracetamol dose in last 4 hours (oral doses only)

- Previous paracetamol dose in last 6 hours (IV doses only)

- Children who do not have a sufficient gag reflex to swallow the measured dose 

- Not to be given to children < 1 month old

- Diagnosed liver failure

#### Precautions

- Impaired hepatic function

- Impaired renal function

#### Pregnancy and Breastfeeding

##### Pregnancy

- Safe to use

##### Breastfeeding

- Safe to use

#### Doses

#### Pyrexia in children

##### Paediatric only

- 15mg/kg – oral liquid given (via syringe)

#### Analgesia 

##### Adult ≥ 12 years

- ≥ 50kg: 
- 2 tablets (1g) PO, or
- 1g IV over 15/60 via burette

- < 50kg: 
- 1 tablet (500mg) PO, or
- 15mg/kg IV over 15/60 via burette
##### Paediatric 

- 15mg/kg PO (liquid), or
- 15mg/kg IV over 15/60 via burette

#### Special Notes

- It is envisaged that the administration of Panadol Liquid will be for children that are post-ictal / post febrile convulsion, or who are likely to have a febrile convulsion and are not responding to non-medical treatment.
- Paracetamol does not necessarily prevent febrile convulsions.
- Active cooling measures should still be carried out, as well as checking for the reason for pyrexia (URTI, meningococcal disease, etc).
- As a general rule, if children are administered paracetamol, they should not be left at home. - Do not exceed 60mg/kg/24hours.
- Children aged 10-12 may take a 500mg tablet if preferred.
